title: PokéAPI Resources, URL API, Pagination Shapes, and Fair-Use Policy · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for pokéapi resources, url api, pagination shapes, and fair-use policy and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for fixed-origin typed PokéAPI request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Hold scheme and authority in trusted configuration and map commands only to enumerated PokéAPI resource paths.
Validate resource kind, numeric identifier or canonical constrained name, and preserve requested versus resolved identity.
Use URL and URLSearchParams for encoded path and limit or offset values while rejecting slash, control, oversized, and unexpected input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 8132 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit fixed-origin PokéAPI URLs, validated pagination shapes, and fair-use request budgets; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Cache every requested resource, bound frequency and concurrency, use responsible client identity, and avoid availability claims.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for cache every requested resource, bound frequency and concurrency, use responsible client identity, and avoid availability claims.
